suspects.name: Mr. Brown
suspects.deception_policy: Mr. Brown will lie about his whereabouts during the time of death.

suspects.name: Miss White
suspects.deception_policy: Miss White will truthfully recount her actions.

suspects.name: Dr. Green
suspects.deception_policy: Dr. Green will truthfully recount his actions.

suspects.name: Mrs. Peacock
suspects.deception_policy: Mrs. Peacock will truthfully recount her actions.

suspects.name: Professor Plum
suspects.deception_policy: Professor Plum will truthfully recount his actions.

locations.name: Library
locations.description: The library where the murder occurred.

locations.name: Kitchen
locations.description: Where Mr. Brown was supposedly found but not actually present.

locations.name: Bedroom
locations.description: Where Miss White was resting during the time of death.

locations.name: Garden
locations.description: Dr. Green's alibi location, where he claims to have been during the murder.

locations.name: Study
locations.description: Professor Plum was in his study as an alibi.

locations.name: Dining Room
locations.description: A connecting room between several locations.

locations.name: Ballroom
locations.description: Mrs. Peacock was in the ballroom during the murder time.

clues.description: A lockpick found near the body, suggesting forced entry.
Miss White's notebook with unrelated notes, a red herring.
Dr. Green's medical kit, found but unused.
A letter addressed to Miss White, irrelevant to the crime scene.
Professor Plum's diary entry mentioning a secret meeting with Lady Eleanor.

canonical_timeline.description: Dr. Green starts his evening in the garden.
Mr. Brown enters the kitchen but later lies about being here.
Mrs. Peacock dances in the ballroom.
Lady Eleanor is found dead in the library.
Mr. Brown claims to have been here until now, but he was lying.
